title: Executive Order 9681—Designating the Honorable Jorge L. Cordova as Acting Judge of the District Court of the United States for Puerto Rico

flags.justification: By analogy to other single-person judicial and administrative designation orders in this corpus, this is presumably a narrow, individual designation.
Presumed narrow, specific designation, consistent with the pattern of similar orders.
A personnel designation would create no compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
An individual judicial designation, not an ongoing authority grant.
No basis to infer any oversight removal from the title alone.
No compliance regime is created by a personnel designation.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. METHODOLOGICAL FLAG: no primary source text is available for this order; the corpus record states only '(this executive order was not published).' This coding is based entirely on the title, by analogy to the many other individual-designation orders in this corpus, all of which have scored cleanly. This is a genuine data-completeness limitation, not a confident, text-grounded coding, and should be flagged for the project record as LOW CONFIDENCE. If additional unpublished orders are encountered in the remaining corpus, consider tracking their frequency separately, since a non-trivial rate of missing primary text would warrant either an alternate sourcing attempt (e.g., the Federal Register's own index of unpublished orders) or explicit exclusion from statistical analysis rather than title-based inference.
